title: Flavorful Umami Middle Eastern Kippers Warak Enab
description: A flavorful and energizing Middle Eastern dish featuring kippers, with a rich umami taste and a satisfying cheese pull, perfect for a weeknight dinner. This egg-free friendly recipe is a twist on the traditional warak enab, with the added depth of mantecare emulsification.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Rinse the grape leaves and blanch them in boiling water for 30 seconds.
In a bowl, mix together the ricotta cheese, feta cheese, garlic, lemon juice, sumac, salt, and black pepper.
Add the mantecare emulsified kippers to the cheese mixture and stir until well combined.
Lay a grape leaf flat and place a tablespoon of the kipper cheese mixture in the center of the leaf.
Fold the stem end over the filling, then fold in the sides and roll the leaf into a neat package.
Repeat with the remaining grape leaves and filling.
Place the warak enab in a baking dish and drizzle with olive oil.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until the grape leaves are tender and the filling is heated through.
Serve hot, garnished with additional sumac and lemon wedges if desired.
